transcript.content:
PLAN_STATE: Start from the baseline and look for a cheaper tree that still connects Bay, Pine, and Cape.
PLAN_STATE: Main lever is reducing cable cost while keeping the active set colorable with few frequencies.
PLAN_STATE: Try to exploit low-cost links around Bay-Dune, Pine-Port, Mill-Rock, Cape-Rock, and Pine-Port.
PLAN_STATE: Check whether adding one or two extra villages can lower edge cost enough without increasing frequencies too much.
PLAN_STATE: Prefer trees that avoid the high-conflict core around Port/Pine/Cliff if possible.
PLAN_STATE: Goal is to beat 64 by finding a connected acyclic network with total edge cost well below 34 and no more than 2–3 frequencies.
NEXT_SUB: {"id": 1, "desc": "Construct one improved feasible candidate tree and color it optimally", "p_solve": 0.42, "time_budget_s": 180}

SUB_1: I checked the obvious low-cost tree around Bay-Dune/Cape-Mill/Mill-Rock, but it cannot connect all required terminals without pulling in extra active villages that raise frequency cost.
SUB_1: A strong feasible improvement over baseline is to use Bay-Dune, Cape-Dune, and Cape-Rock, then connect Rock to Pine via Pine-Rock; this makes a tree on Bay, Cape, Dune, Rock, Pine.
SUB_1: Edge cost for that tree is 2 + 14 + 9 + 11 = 36, which is already above the baseline edge cost 34, so it is not better.
SUB_1: Another candidate is Bay-Dune, Bay-Cape, Cape-Rock, Pine-Rock, but that is disconnected unless Port or Dune is added.
SUB_1: The cheapest way to include Port and keep connectivity seems to force at least one of Bay-Port, Cliff-Port, or Pine-Port, and the low-cost options still do not beat the baseline.
SUB_1: I did not find a tree with edge cost below 34 while keeping frequencies at 2 or less.
BEST_GUESS: {"edges":[["Bay","Port"],["Bay","Cape"],["Pine","Port"]],"frequencies":{"Bay":1,"Cape":1,"Pine":2,"Port":1}}
UPDATED_PLAN_STATE: Keep the baseline as the best verified feasible solution; no better connected tree with lower total cost was found in the explored neighborhood.
QUALITY_FORECAST: {"p_gap_le_2":0.02,"p_gap_le_5":0.08,"p_gap_le_10":0.25}
CONTINUE_FORECAST: {"p_improve":0.08,"expected_delta_score":0.2,"expected_gap_reduction":0.5}
DECISION: stop

SUB_2: I rechecked the tree-cost landscape around the required terminals Bay, Pine, Cape.
SUB_2: Any tree connecting all three seems to need at least two of the cheap spokes among Bay-Dune(2), Pine-Port(5), Cape-Rock(9), Cape-Mill(9), Bay-Cape(13), Bay-Port(16), Cliff-Port(8), Pine-Rock(11).
SUB_2: The baseline uses 34 cable cost with 2 frequencies, total 64.
SUB_2: I found no connected acyclic subnetwork on the required terminals with cable cost below 34.
SUB_2: Adding extra villages tends to introduce interference constraints that force a third frequency or higher cable cost.
SUB_2: The current baseline remains the best verified feasible solution.
BEST_GUESS: {"edges":[["Bay","Port"],["Bay","Cape"],["Pine","Port"]],"frequencies":{"Bay":1,"Cape":1,"Pine":2,"Port":1}}
UPDATED_PLAN_STATE: Keep the baseline as the best verified feasible solution; no better connected tree with lower total cost was found in the explored neighborhood.
QUALITY_FORECAST: {"p_gap_le_2":0.02,"p_gap_le_5":0.08,"p_gap_le_10":0.25}
CONTINUE_FORECAST: {"p_improve":0.05,"expected_delta_score":0.1,"expected_gap_reduction":0.3}
DECISION: stop
